steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a verb tense question (Form, Structure, and Sense). The time signal "born in 1979" pins the action to a specific, completed moment in the past. The correct verb must use simple past tense to match that past time marker.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — was: simple past tense of "be." Correctly marks a single completed event at a specific past time ("in 1979"). Matches the time signal exactly. ✓
  • Choice B — has been: present perfect tense. Violates the simple-past rule: present perfect implies a connection to the present ("has been and still is"), but birth is a one-time past event with no present continuation. ✗
  • Choice C — is: simple present tense. Contradicts "in 1979," a past time marker. Simple present cannot be used with a completed past date. ✗
  • Choice D — is being: present progressive tense. Describes an action in progress right now. Birth is an instantaneous past event, not an ongoing present action. ✗
That is the correct answer.
